chapter_title.ar: بَابُ دُخُولِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَ زِيَارَةِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ الدُّعَاءِ عِنْدَ قَبْرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 216 – Entering Al-Medina and Ziyarah of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, and the supplication at his<sup>saww</sup> grave

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/263)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/263)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَمَرِّ فِي مُؤَخَّرِ مَسْجِدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ لَا أُسَلِّمُ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَ لَمْ يَكُنْ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) يَصْنَعُ ذَلِكَ قُلْتُ فَيَدْخُلُ الْمَسْجِدَ فَيُسَلِّمُ مِنْ بَعِيدٍ لَا يَدْنُو مِنَ الْقَبْرِ فَقَالَ لَا قَالَ سَلِّمْ عَلَيْهِ حِينَ تَدْخُلُ وَ حِينَ تَخْرُجُ وَ مِنْ بَعِيدٍ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Safwan Bin Yahya who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about the passing by at the end of the Masjid of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> and not greeting upon the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> did not happen to do that’. I said, ‘So he<sup>asws</sup> would enter the Masjid, and he<sup>asws</sup> would greet from afar, not approaching the grave?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, (but) He<sup>asws</sup> said greetings upon him<sup>saww</sup> while entering and when exiting, and from afar’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Safwan ibn Yahya who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about passing behind the Masjid of the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, without offering al-Salam to the Holy Prophet. He (the Imam) replied, ’Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would not do so.’ I then asked, ’Would he enter the Masjid and offer al-Salam without approaching the grave?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘No, he, however, would offer al-Salam when entering and when leaving (the Masjid) from a distance.’”
